public String baseNeg2(int n) {
    if (n == 0) return "0";
    StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder();
    while (n != 0) {
        int remainder = n % (-2);
        if (remainder < 0) {
            remainder += 2;
            n = (n - remainder) / (-2);
        } else {
            n = n / (-2);
        }
        sb.append(remainder);
    }
    return sb.reverse().toString();
}

solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public String baseNeg2(int n) {
        if (n == 0) return "0";
        StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder();
        while (n != 0) {
            int remainder = n % (-2);
            if (remainder < 0) {
                remainder += 2;
                n = (n - remainder) / (-2);
            } else {
                n = n / (-2);
            }
            sb.append(remainder);
        }
        return sb.reverse().toString();
    }
}
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log n)
space complexity: O(log n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The algorithm efficiently converts the integer to base -2 by iteratively computing remainders and adjusting for negative remainders, ensuring optimal performance with logarithmic time and space complexity.
